contextual.title: Evaluating the Link Between Neighborhood Environments and Obesity Among African American Women
contextual.text:
Summary: African American women have higher rates of obesity than women of any other racial or ethnic group in the United States. Obesity can have many causes, including genetic and environmental factors. This study will examine how neighborhood environments influence the occurrence of obesity among African American women.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 Participating in the BWHS study 

 Residing in New York, Los Angeles, or Chicago
Exclusion criteria:
